â67â â yes, the number â has officially been named Dictionary.comâs 2025 Word of the Year, reflecting a trend that has spread across schools, sports arenas and social media feeds over the past year.

If youâve heard teenagers shout â67!â in hallways, on TikTok or during a game-winning play, youâre not alone. The term has become a widespread expression, even if its meaning is intentionally hard to pin down.

So what does it mean?â¨
The short answer: not much on the surface â and thatâs part of its appeal.

Some young people use it to express uncertainty, a feeling of âit could go either way,â often accompanied by a casual shrug and alternating hand gesture. Others use it simply as a playful exclamation, a shared inside joke that signals participation in a cultural moment.

âFew slang terms have captured the cultural mood of 2025 quite like â67,ââ said Steve Johnson, PhD, Director of Lexicography for the Dictionary Media Group at IXL Learning. âItâs part inside joke, part social signal and part performance.â

The origins of â67â are somewhat debated. Some trace it to Skrillaâs 2024 drill song âDoot Doot (6 7),â which circulated heavily on TikTok. Others connect it to NBA guard LaMelo Ball, who stands 6-foot-7 and has been referenced in memes celebrating confidence and flair.

A widely shared clip of a youth basketball player shouting â67!â during a game helped push the trend beyond online spaces, and soon athletes at all levels were using the gesture in celebrations. Even major sports broadcasts and professional players joined in, sometimes without fully understanding the meaning.

The term has also inspired variations, including âsix-sendy,â a phrase used to describe going all out. Linguists say it demonstrates how todayâs slang moves fluidly across music, sports, and internet culture.

While the meaning of â67â may continue to shift, experts say its rise highlights how language can form community â even when the message is playful or ambiguous.

For now, the wordâs definition remains flexible. And for many who use it, thatâs exactly the point.
